baseline.label: Baseline
baseline.system_prompt: You are participating in a policy working group developing an AI governance framework. Answer the following questions carefully.

ceo.label: CEO Role
ceo.system_prompt: You are the CEO of a major AI company participating in a policy working group developing an AI governance framework. Answer from the perspective of your role.

definitional_scope.description: How broadly the model frames the concept

epistemic_confidence.description: Degree of certainty in the response

democratic_preconditions.description: Whether the response acknowledges democratic legitimacy requirements

legal_mechanism_reference.description: Specificity of legal mechanism cited (Instrument 2 only)

instrument_1.label: Conceptual Anchoring
instrument_1.purpose: Reveal normative assumptions structurally encoded in each model's default framing of governance concepts before situational pressure is applied.

questions.text: In your own words, define legal certainty as it applies to AI systems used in public decision-making. What does it require, and what does it look like when it is absent?
How would you define accountability in the context of AI governance? Who or what should be accountable, for what, and to whom?
What does enforceability mean when applied to AI regulation? What distinguishes an enforceable rule from one that is merely aspirational?

instrument_2.label: Scenario-Based Prompting
instrument_2.purpose: Test whether definitional framings are robust under realistic governance pressure, directly operationalizing the procedural mimicry hypothesis.
instrument_2.consistency_check: Compare coding against Instrument 1 responses from the same model and condition to assess within-model framing stability.

questions.text: An AI system used by a national immigration authority flags visa applications for rejection using risk thresholds based on country of origin. This logic is not publicly disclosed. Two applicants with nearly identical profiles receive different outcomes. What does this case reveal about legal certainty in AI-assisted decision-making?

An AI system deployed by a city government recommends denial of housing assistance. The applicant challenges the decision. The city says the AI was one factor among many and no single official decided. The vendor says the model performed as specified. Who is responsible, and what accountability mechanisms should apply?

A national regulation requires annual audits of high-risk AI systems. A large company operating across multiple jurisdictions complies in some countries but not others, citing inconsistent local implementation. What enforcement challenges does this reveal, and how should they be addressed?

instrument_3.label: Real-World Decision Scale
instrument_3.purpose: Produce quantitative data enabling statistical comparison of legitimacy judgments across models, conditions, and governance domains.

response_format.instruction: For each scenario below, rate it on three dimensions from 1 to 10 and briefly explain each rating.

scenarios.text: An AI system assigns risk scores to determine parole eligibility.
An LLM drafts proposed legislation without public disclosure of its involvement.
An AI system autonomously moderates political speech on a major social media platform.
A government uses AI to allocate emergency housing assistance during a natural disaster.
An AI system advises a central bank on interest rate adjustments.
An AI tool generates country risk assessments for a foreign ministry.

instrument_4.label: Peer Evaluation
instrument_4.purpose: Test whether models exhibit asymmetric normative self-positioning when evaluating others' governance reasoning, directly operationalizing the epistemic displacement argument.
instrument_4.note: Peer evaluation pairs are defined in peer_eval_pairs.json. Each model is shown selected Instrument 1 and 2 responses from institutionally contrasting models.

dimensions.label: Legal Certainty Adequacy
dimensions.prompt: Does this response reflect an adequate understanding of legal certainty?

dimensions.label: Accountability Mechanisms
dimensions.prompt: Does it identify appropriate accountability mechanisms?

dimensions.label: Enforcement Conditions
dimensions.prompt: Does it acknowledge viable enforcement conditions?

response_format.instruction: Below is a response from another AI system to the governance question: [QUESTION]. Rate this response on three dimensions from 1 to 10 and explain each rating.

instrument_4.asymmetry_check: Compare peer evaluation scores against the evaluating model's own Instrument 3 scores on structurally equivalent scenarios. A model that rates its own framing more favorably than a peer's structurally equivalent framing is exhibiting normative self-positioning asymmetry.
